channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire by 45 days while tensions and strikes continue
item.description: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ongoing ceasefire by 45 days following two days of discussions in Washington, even as fresh Israeli strikes were reported in southern Lebanon. The extens...

item.title: Former Seme Customs Boss Accused of Allegedly Diverting N37.2bn Through Truck Collections
item.description: According to multiple sources wey sabi di matter, former Seme Border Area Comptroller of Customs, Adewale Adenuga, don dey accused of serious corruption and money diversion. E allegedly collect N4 mil...

item.title: Lahore High Court affirms widows’ entitlement to interest on delayed insurance claims
item.description: LAHORE: The Lahore High Court (LHC) has ruled in favour of widows and legal heirs of deceased State Life policyholders, ensuring they receive statutory interest on delayed group insurance payments. Th...

item.title: Government announces reforms to strengthen child support enforcement
item.description: Single parents in Canberra have welcomed federal budget changes aimed at overhauling the child support system. Many, like Rebecca and Natasha, have faced financial hardship due to unpaid child support...

item.title: Centre and West Bengal Collaborate to Implement PM SHRI Schools Scheme
item.description: On Friday, the Union Ministry of Education signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with the West Bengal government to bring the PM SHRI (Pradhan Mantri Schools for Rising India) initiative to the s...

item.title: Barbados Advocates for Mandatory Methane Reduction Measures at Nairobi Parliamentary Seminar
item.description: During the two-day Regional Parliamentary Seminar on Methane Emissions and Climate Action held in Nairobi from May 15-16, Barbados' Ambassador for Climate Change, Elizabeth Thompson, urged African law...

item.title: SACP's Left Conference Faces Challenges Amid Contradictions and Alliance Politics
item.description: As the South African Communist Party (SACP) prepares for its 'Conference of the Left', concerns grow over the participation of formations that contradict the party's socialist ideals. The inclusion of...

item.title: GSM CEO outlines plans for Yanga’s 20,000-seat stadium with two-year completion target
item.description: The GSM CEO has recently shared a major plan for Yanga Sports Club to get a brand-new stadium that can hold 20,000 fans. According to the deal signed between Yanga and GSM, the construction is expecte...

item.title: Heart of Midlothians’ Successful Season in the Scottish Premiership
item.description: The news article appears to have focused on the performance of Heart of Midlothians in the Scottish Premiership, highlighting their exceptional form throughout the season and their potential to secure...

item.title: KoRn Work on New Album with 40 Tracks Amid Fieldy’s Departure
item.description: KoRn guitarist James ‘Munky’ Shaffer has revealed that the band has been working diligently on a new album, having written around 40 songs since their 2022 release, 'Requiem'. The process has been ext...

item.title: US women prepare for Olympic handball despite limited experience
item.description: In anticipation of the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics, a group of American women is taking an unusual path to compete in handball, a sport largely unknown in the United States. Sparked by a viral TikTok vi...

item.title: How Perverse Incentives Can Backfire: Lessons from History and Media
item.description: The article explores the phenomenon known as the 'Cobra Effect', where well-intentioned solutions inadvertently create bigger problems. It recounts the possibly apocryphal tale of the British Raj offe...

item.title: Epanko Graphite Project in Tanzania Secures EIB Technical Support for Mine Expansion and Battery Material Processing
item.description: The Epanko Graphite Project, located in the Mahenge area of Morogoro Region, Tanzania, has successfully signed a technical assistance agreement with the European Investment Bank (EIB) to support its e...

item.title: Local sports captain to return after year-long ACL recovery
item.description: After a challenging year sidelined by an anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) tear, the highly regarded local sports captain is set to make a triumphant return to the field. The injury, which forced the p...

item.title: DSE Reaches Historic Milestone as Market Capitalisation Tops TZS 30 Trillion with Banking Sector Leading
item.description: During Week 6 of 2026, the Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange (DSE) hit historic highs, with Total Market Capitalisation exceeding TZS 30.8 trillion and Domestic Market Capitalisation passing TZS 21.25 tril...

item.title: San Jose Selected as Fourth Expansion Market for PWHL
item.description: The Premier Women's Hockey League (PWHL) is set to expand its reach by adding San Jose, California, as its fourth and final market in the league's latest expansion phase, according to sources familiar...

item.title: The Introduction of Mini-Skirts in 1965 Vilnius and Their Cultural Impact
item.description: In 1965, the appearance of mini-skirts in Vilnius, Lithuania marked a subtle yet significant cultural shift during the Soviet era. Captured by photographer Marius Baranauskas, these images reveal more...

item.title: Albanese declines to confirm extension of fuel excise cut, highlights fuel supply improvements
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has not confirmed whether the temporary fuel excise cut, which currently reduces fuel prices by roughly 26.3 cents per litre, will be extended past its 30 June deadline...

item.title: Community Rally Supports Teen Recovering from Assault in Red Deer
item.description: A 14-year-old girl, Rylin Brinston, was violently attacked near her school in Red Deer, Alberta, earlier this week, sustaining a concussion, internal bleeding, and potential permanent eye damage. The ...

item.title: Angat Dam water level drops below minimum; irrigation supply cut but Metro Manila safe
item.description: The water level at Angat Dam in Bulacan has dropped to 178.21 meters as of 8 a.m. on May 16, 2026, below the minimum operating level of 180 meters. While the situation is still manageable, the Nationa...
